---
title: "John Baldessari — Exhibition Catalogue"
subtitle: "Sprengel Museum Hannover"
description: "Catalogue of works by John Baldessari shown at the Sprengel Museum Hannover, sourced from OCR-processed exhibition catalogue (scan-2)."
date: today
execute:
  echo: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: load-xml
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from pathlib import Path

XML = Path("catalogues/scan-2/catalogue-items.xml")
tree = ET.parse(XML)
root = tree.getroot()

def t(el, tag):
    child = el.find(tag)
    return child.text.strip() if child is not None and child.text else ""

meta = root.find("meta")
exhibition_title = t(meta, "exhibition_title")
venue            = t(meta, "venue")
creator_name     = t(meta, "creator_name")
creator_qid      = t(meta, "creator_qid")

items = []
for item in root.findall("items/item"):
    dim = item.find("dimensions")
    h = t(dim, "height_cm") if dim is not None else ""
    w = t(dim, "width_cm")  if dim is not None else ""
    d = t(dim, "depth_cm")  if dim is not None else ""
    dim_str = ""
    if h and w:
        dim_str = f"{h} × {w}" + (f" × {d} cm" if d else " cm")
    items.append({
        "title":           t(item, "title"),
        "date":            t(item, "date"),
        "object_type":     t(item, "object_type"),
        "object_type_qid": t(item, "object_type_qid"),
        "materials":       t(item, "materials"),
        "dimensions":      dim_str,
        "catalog_number":  t(item, "catalog_number"),
        "inventory_number":t(item, "inventory_number"),
        "location":        t(item, "location"),
        "notes":           t(item, "notes"),
    })

print(f"Loaded {len(items)} catalogue entries")
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Creator:** [{{< meta title >}}](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q312793) (Q312793) &ensp;|&ensp;
**Venue:** [Sprengel Museum Hannover](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q510144) &ensp;|&ensp;
**Source:** OCR-processed exhibition catalogue, `catalogues/scan-2/`
:::

## Works in the Exhibition

```{python}
#| label: cards

# Group items by object type for display
from collections import Counter
from IPython.display import HTML
type_counts = Counter(i["object_type"] or "unclassified" for i in items)

def wd_badge(qid, label, colour="secondary"):
    if qid:
        return (f'<a href="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/{qid}" target="_blank" '
                f'class="badge bg-{colour} text-decoration-none me-1">{label}</a>')
    return f'<span class="badge bg-{colour} me-1">{label}</span>'

cards_html = '<div class="row row-cols-1 row-cols-md-2 row-cols-lg-3 g-3 mt-1">\n'

for item in items:
    title = item["title"] or "(untitled)"
    date  = item["date"]
    otype = item["object_type"]
    oqid  = item["object_type_qid"]
    mats  = item["materials"]
    dims  = item["dimensions"]
    cat_n = item["catalog_number"]
    inv_n = item["inventory_number"]
    notes = item["notes"]

    type_badge = wd_badge(oqid, otype, "primary") if otype else ""
    cat_badge  = (f'<span class="badge bg-light text-dark border me-1">cat. {cat_n}</span>'
                  if cat_n else "")

    meta_lines = []
    if date:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Date:</b> {date}")
    if mats:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Materials:</b> {mats}")
    if dims:   meta_lines.append(f"<b>Dimensions:</b> {dims}")
    if inv_n:  meta_lines.append(f"<b>Inventory:</b> {inv_n}")
    if notes:  meta_lines.append(f"<b>Note:</b> {notes}")
    meta_html = "<br>".join(meta_lines)

    cards_html += f"""  <div class="col">
    <div class="card h-100 shadow-sm">
      <div class="card-body">
        <h6 class="card-title fw-semibold">{title}</h6>
        <p class="card-text mb-1">{type_badge}{cat_badge}</p>
        <p class="card-text small text-muted">{meta_html}</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
"""

cards_html += "</div>\n"
HTML(cards_html)
```

---

## Summary Statistics

```{python}
#| label: summary
from IPython.display import HTML
import pandas as pd

# Object type breakdown
type_df = (pd.DataFrame(list(type_counts.items()), columns=["Object Type", "Count"])
             .sort_values("Count", ascending=False)
             .reset_index(drop=True))
type_df.index = range(1, len(type_df) + 1)

# Date breakdown (year only)
year_counts = Counter()
for i in items:
    d = i["date"]
    if d:
        year = d[:4] if len(d) >= 4 else d
        year_counts[year] += 1

year_df = (pd.DataFrame(list(year_counts.items()), columns=["Year / Period", "Count"])
             .sort_values("Year / Period")
             .reset_index(drop=True))
year_df.index = range(1, len(year_df) + 1)

html = (
    '<div class="row">'
    '<div class="col-md-5">'
    + type_df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0, escape=False)
    + '</div><div class="col-md-4 offset-md-1">'
    + year_df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-bordered", border=0, escape=False)
    + '</div></div>'
)
HTML(html)
```

---

## Full Catalogue Table

```{python}
#| label: full-table
df = pd.DataFrame(items)
df.columns = ["Title", "Date", "Type", "Type QID", "Materials",
              "Dimensions", "Cat. No.", "Inv. No.", "Location", "Notes"]
df = df.drop(columns=["Type QID"])
df.index = range(1, len(df) + 1)

# Truncate very long material strings for readability
df["Materials"] = df["Materials"].str[:60].where(
    df["Materials"].str.len() <= 60,
    df["Materials"].str[:60] + "…"
)

HTML(df.to_html(classes="table table-sm table-hover", border=0, escape=False))
```
